2026-04-27T05:02:02.4014900Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-27T05:02:02.4015465Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-27T05:02:02.4015885Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-27T05:02:02.4016242Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-27T05:02:02.4016552Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-27T05:02:02.4016797Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-27T05:02:02.4016966Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-27T05:02:02.4017152Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-27T05:02:02.4017329Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-27T05:02:02.4017563Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-27T05:02:02.4018056Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-27T05:02:02.4018320Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-27T05:03:17.4270730Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-27T05:03:03.673146037Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58686,\"title\":\"[mgmt-emitter] @@clientName on a non-leaf ARM resource drops parent\\u0026#39;s child-collection accessor\",\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nWhen `@@clientName` is applied to an ARM resource that is itself a parent of other ARM resources, the C# mgmt emitter drops the **parent\\u0026#39;s** child-collection accessor (`GetXxx`/`GetXxxs`) entirely. The renamed child resource and its collection are emitted correctly, but there is no way to obtain the collection from the parent — making the renamed type effectively unreachable through normal navigation.\\n\\nSibling resources that are leaves (no children of their own) rename correctly. The bug is specific to renaming a \\u0026#34;middle-of-tree\\u0026#34; resource.\\n\\n## Generator versions\\n\\n- `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt@1.0.0-alpha.20260423.3`\\n- `@typespec/http-client-csharp@1.0.0-alpha.20260414.6`\\n- `@typespec/compiler@1.11.0`\\n\\n## Real-world example\\n\\nNetApp client.tsp:\\n\\n```typespec\\n@@clientName(Volume, \\u0026#34;NetAppVolume\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\nResource tree: `CapacityPool` (parent) → `Volume` → `{Snapshot, Bucket, SubvolumeInfo, VolumeQuotaRule, RansomwareReport}`.\\n\\nAfter regeneration:\\n- ✅ `Generated/NetAppVolumeResource.cs` is emitted with all child accessors (`GetNetAppBuckets`, `GetNetAppSubvolumeInfos`, `GetNetAppVolumeQuotaRules`, `GetRansomwareReports`).\\n- ✅ `Generated/NetAppVolumeCollection.cs` is emitted.\\n- ❌ `Generated/CapacityPoolResource.cs` contains **zero** references to `Volume` or `NetAppVolume`. No `GetNetAppVolumes()` / `GetNetAppVolume(string)` accessor is emitted.\\n\\n```bash\\n$ grep -i volume Generated/CapacityPoolResource.cs\\n# (no output)\\n\\n# Compare with sibling Cache (also @parentResource(CapacityPool)) which has no children:\\n$ grep -nE \\u0026#34;virtual.*Get(NetApp)?Cache\\u0026#34; Generated/CapacityPoolResource.cs\\n685:    public virtual NetAppCacheCollection GetNetAppCaches()\\n696:    public virtual async Task\\u0026gt; GetNetAppCacheAsync(...)\\n709:    public virtual Response GetNetAppCache(...)\\n```\\n\\n`Cache` is also renamed via `@@clientName(Cache, \\u0026#34;NetAppCache\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;)` but works because it has no children of its own.\\n\\n## Minimal TypeSpec repro\\n\\n```typespec\\nimport \\u0026#34;@typespec/rest\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@typespec/versioning\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager\\u0026#34;;\\n\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing TypeSpec.Http;\\nusing TypeSpec.Versioning;\\n\\n@armProviderNamespace\\n@service(#{ title: \\u0026#34;Contoso\\u0026#34; })\\n@versioned(Versions)\\nnamespace Contoso;\\n\\nenum Versions { v1: \\u0026#34;2024-01-01\\u0026#34; }\\n\\n// Top-level (root) resource\\nmodel Foo is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\u0026#34;fooName\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\u0026#34;foos\\u0026#34;) @path name: string;\\n}\\nmodel FooProperties { displayName?: string; }\\n\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface Foos extends TrackedResourceOperations {}\\n\\n// Middle resource (child of Foo, parent of Baz). This is what we want to rename.\\n@parentResource(Foo)\\nmodel Bar is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\u0026#34;barName\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\u0026#34;bars\\u0026#34;) @path name: string;\\n}\\nmodel BarProperties { description?: string; }\\n\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface Bars extends TrackedResourceOperations {}\\n\\n// Leaf resource (child of Bar) — its existence triggers the bug.\\n@parentResource(Bar)\\nmodel Baz is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\u0026#34;bazName\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\u0026#34;bazs\\u0026#34;) @path name: string;\\n}\\nmodel BazProperties { value?: string; }\\n\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface Bazs extends TrackedResourceOperations {}\\n```\\n\\n`client.tsp`:\\n\\n```typespec\\nimport \\u0026#34;./main.tsp\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-client-generator-core\\u0026#34;;\\nusing Azure.ClientGenerator.Core;\\n\\n// This rename causes FooResource.GetBars() / GetMyBars() to disappear.\\n@@clientName(Bar, \\u0026#34;MyBar\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### Expected\\n\\n`Generated/FooResource.cs` should contain a `GetMyBars()` accessor (and `GetMyBar(string)` / `GetMyBarAsync(string)`) that returns `MyBarCollection`.\\n\\n### Actual\\n\\n`FooResource.cs` has no accessor for the renamed `Bar`/`MyBar` collection at all. `MyBarResource`/`MyBarCollection`/`MyBarData` are emitted correctly with their child `GetBazs()` accessors, but cannot be reached from `FooResource` via normal navigation.\\n\\nIf `Baz` is removed (so `Bar` becomes a leaf), the rename works as expected.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nDo not rename middle resources. For NetApp, we kept `Volume` unrenamed (generated as `VolumeResource`/`VolumeCollection`/`VolumeData`) and added deprecated `NetAppVolumeResource`/`NetAppVolumeCollection`/`NetAppVolumeData` shim classes in `src/Custom/` for source compatibility with the GA-shipped names. This is undesirable because the GA name (`NetAppVolume*`) is the more meaningful one.\\n\\n## Hypothesis\\n\\nThe accessor-emission step on the parent resource appears to be keyed off something tied to the renamed resource\\u0026#39;s own child-resource handling. Renaming a leaf resource works; renaming a non-leaf resource with the same decorator does not.\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58686\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"assignees\":[\"live1206\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-27T05:00:37Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-27T05:02:27Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-27T05:03:17.4313888Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-27T05:03:03.849160423Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58686,\"title\":\"[mgmt-emitter] @@clientName on a non-leaf ARM resource drops parent\\u0026#39;s child-collection accessor\",\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nWhen `@@clientName` is applied to an ARM resource that is itself a parent of other ARM resources, the C# mgmt emitter drops the **parent\\u0026#39;s** child-collection accessor (`GetXxx`/`GetXxxs`) entirely. The renamed child resource and its collection are emitted correctly, but there is no way to obtain the collection from the parent — making the renamed type effectively unreachable through normal navigation.\\n\\nSibling resources that are leaves (no children of their own) rename correctly. The bug is specific to renaming a \\u0026#34;middle-of-tree\\u0026#34; resource.\\n\\n## Generator versions\\n\\n- `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt@1.0.0-alpha.20260423.3`\\n- `@typespec/http-client-csharp@1.0.0-alpha.20260414.6`\\n- `@typespec/compiler@1.11.0`\\n\\n## Real-world example\\n\\nNetApp client.tsp:\\n\\n```typespec\\n@@clientName(Volume, \\u0026#34;NetAppVolume\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\nResource tree: `CapacityPool` (parent) → `Volume` → `{Snapshot, Bucket, SubvolumeInfo, VolumeQuotaRule, RansomwareReport}`.\\n\\nAfter regeneration:\\n- ✅ `Generated/NetAppVolumeResource.cs` is emitted with all child accessors (`GetNetAppBuckets`, `GetNetAppSubvolumeInfos`, `GetNetAppVolumeQuotaRules`, `GetRansomwareReports`).\\n- ✅ `Generated/NetAppVolumeCollection.cs` is emitted.\\n- ❌ `Generated/CapacityPoolResource.cs` contains **zero** references to `Volume` or `NetAppVolume`. No `GetNetAppVolumes()` / `GetNetAppVolume(string)` accessor is emitted.\\n\\n```bash\\n$ grep -i volume Generated/CapacityPoolResource.cs\\n# (no output)\\n\\n# Compare with sibling Cache (also @parentResource(CapacityPool)) which has no children:\\n$ grep -nE \\u0026#34;virtual.*Get(NetApp)?Cache\\u0026#34; Generated/CapacityPoolResource.cs\\n685:    public virtual NetAppCacheCollection GetNetAppCaches()\\n696:    public virtual async Task\\u0026gt; GetNetAppCacheAsync(...)\\n709:    public virtual Response GetNetAppCache(...)\\n```\\n\\n`Cache` is also renamed via `@@clientName(Cache, \\u0026#34;NetAppCache\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;)` but works because it has no children of its own.\\n\\n## Minimal TypeSpec repro\\n\\n```typespec\\nimport \\u0026#34;@typespec/rest\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@typespec/versioning\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager\\u0026#34;;\\n\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing TypeSpec.Http;\\nusing TypeSpec.Versioning;\\n\\n@armProviderNamespace\\n@service(#{ title: \\u0026#34;Contoso\\u0026#34; })\\n@versioned(Versions)\\nnamespace Contoso;\\n\\nenum Versions { v1: \\u0026#34;2024-01-01\\u0026#34; }\\n\\n// Top-level (root) resource\\nmodel Foo is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\u0026#34;fooName\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\u0026#34;foos\\u0026#34;) @path name: string;\\n}\\nmodel FooProperties { displayName?: string; }\\n\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface Foos extends TrackedResourceOperations {}\\n\\n// Middle resource (child of Foo, parent of Baz). This is what we want to rename.\\n@parentResource(Foo)\\nmodel Bar is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\u0026#34;barName\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\u0026#34;bars\\u0026#34;) @path name: string;\\n}\\nmodel BarProperties { description?: string; }\\n\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface Bars extends TrackedResourceOperations {}\\n\\n// Leaf resource (child of Bar) — its existence triggers the bug.\\n@parentResource(Bar)\\nmodel Baz is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\u0026#34;bazName\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\u0026#34;bazs\\u0026#34;) @path name: string;\\n}\\nmodel BazProperties { value?: string; }\\n\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface Bazs extends TrackedResourceOperations {}\\n```\\n\\n`client.tsp`:\\n\\n```typespec\\nimport \\u0026#34;./main.tsp\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-client-generator-core\\u0026#34;;\\nusing Azure.ClientGenerator.Core;\\n\\n// This rename causes FooResource.GetBars() / GetMyBars() to disappear.\\n@@clientName(Bar, \\u0026#34;MyBar\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### Expected\\n\\n`Generated/FooResource.cs` should contain a `GetMyBars()` accessor (and `GetMyBar(string)` / `GetMyBarAsync(string)`) that returns `MyBarCollection`.\\n\\n### Actual\\n\\n`FooResource.cs` has no accessor for the renamed `Bar`/`MyBar` collection at all. `MyBarResource`/`MyBarCollection`/`MyBarData` are emitted correctly with their child `GetBazs()` accessors, but cannot be reached from `FooResource` via normal navigation.\\n\\nIf `Baz` is removed (so `Bar` becomes a leaf), the rename works as expected.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nDo not rename middle resources. For NetApp, we kept `Volume` unrenamed (generated as `VolumeResource`/`VolumeCollection`/`VolumeData`) and added deprecated `NetAppVolumeResource`/`NetAppVolumeCollection`/`NetAppVolumeData` shim classes in `src/Custom/` for source compatibility with the GA-shipped names. This is undesirable because the GA name (`NetAppVolume*`) is the more meaningful one.\\n\\n## Hypothesis\\n\\nThe accessor-emission step on the parent resource appears to be keyed off something tied to the renamed resource\\u0026#39;s own child-resource handling.
